name: Unicare Transport Svc
districts_or_customers: BBB-listed medical transport clients across Southeastern U.S.; no confirmed school district contracts
recent_news: No M&A news found; BBB profile shows 16 years in operation, not BBB-accredited, 'Not Rated' (2026).
fit_notes: Poor fit for Summit. Unicare is a long-distance non-emergency medical transport / ambulance operator with EMT/paramedic staffing, not a school-bus contractor; no evidence of school district contracts. Out of scope unless Summit expands into SPED/NEMT verticals.

name: Levy School Bus Co
principals: Warren Levy Jr. (current head of Management Team, 4th gen)
Warren M. Levy (3rd gen, prior leader)
Michelle Larrabee (per source data, role unverified)
districts_or_customers: Salisbury Township School District (via Paragon Transit acquisition)
Other Bucks/Montgomery/Lehigh County private/parochial schools — ~20,000 students
recent_news: 2021: Acquired Paragon Transit (Salisbury SD routes). 2016: Acquired Lazer Limousine, expanding into charter/airport services. Celebrating ~99 years in 2026.
fit_notes: Strong Summit tuck-in candidate: 4th-generation family-owned PA contractor with ~250 buses, ~20,000 students, multiple SE PA district contracts (Quakertown anchor), and a recent acquisitive track record. Multi-generational ownership and ~100-year milestone create potential succession/liquidity event window.

name: A J Myers & Sons Inc
recent_news: Family-owned and operated for 75+ years (2025-2026 milestone). No public M&A activity identified.
fit_notes: High-priority Summit target: ~400-bus family-owned Western PA operator with diversified Pittsburgh-metro district book (Pittsburgh Public, Bethel Park, Butler, etc.) plus wheelchair/SPED capability. 75-year, multi-generational ownership signals plausible succession-driven sale window. Strong geographic complement to Levy in eastern PA.

name: Cyr Bus Line
owner_or_parent: independent (John T. Cyr & Sons, Inc. — Cyr family, 4th generation)
principals: Mike Cyr (Co-owner, 4th gen, son of Joe)
Becky (Cyr) Whitmore (Co-owner, 4th gen, daughter of Joe)
districts_or_customers: 13 Maine school districts across 25 communities (Bangor, Old Town, Caribou, Houlton, Presque Isle, Mars Hill, Medway, Monticello, Sherman, Bridgewater, Howland, Oakfield, etc.)
Charter clients: University of Maine, Husson University, Colby College
recent_news: 2022: Celebrated 110th anniversary (Bus & Motorcoach News feature). Featured in Maine DOE Clean School Bus electric bus case study; participating in Maine's EV school bus program.
fit_notes: Highly attractive Summit tuck-in despite ME being a non-flagged state: 250 school buses + 22 motorcoaches, 13-district contract base across Northern/Central Maine, dominant regional moat, EV-bus participation. High succession signal — founder's grandson Joe Cyr has stepped back, 4th-gen siblings Mike Cyr and Becky Whitmore are co-owners (classic family-business liquidity setup). Worth pursuing as a Northeast platform/anchor.

name: Southwest Bus Svc Inc
principals: Derrick Boxrucker (President per industry citations; also President, Wisconsin School Bus Association)
recent_news: April 2026: Derrick Boxrucker (as WSBA President) quoted on Wisconsin bill allowing audio/video recording on school buses. No M&A activity identified.
fit_notes: Small but solid SW Wisconsin family operator with 6 contracted school districts in the Driftless region. Sub-scale alone (~25 employees, ~$1M revenue per source data is likely understated given 6 districts), but interesting as a Wisconsin entry point or bolt-on to a future Midwest platform. Boxrucker leadership active in state association — relationship-driven approach required.
